title: Secretary S Day

jokes.text: Wait, I'm sitting on them. This is awesome.
jokes.setup: Kevin looking for his Girl Scout cookies
jokes.punchline: He discovers he's been sitting on them the whole time
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that fits Kevin's oblivious character perfectly

jokes.text: What's the difference between a chimichanga, a chalupa and a tostada? Call me back ASAP. It's urgent.
jokes.setup: Kevin leaving what sounds like an important voicemail
jokes.punchline: It's about Mexican food terminology marked as urgent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin character moment - taking food distinctions with absurd seriousness

jokes.text: Wait, this isn't funny. I don't talk like that.
jokes.setup: Others are doing Cookie Monster impressions of Kevin
jokes.punchline: Kevin's defensive reaction to the parody
jokes.explanation: Kevin's obliviousness to how he actually sounds creates the humor

jokes.text: Say, 'Me eat cookie.' No. I won't say it.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to get Kevin to play along with Cookie Monster impression
jokes.punchline: Kevin's stubborn refusal despite the simplicity
jokes.explanation: Kevin's dignity in the face of mockery creates cringe comedy

jokes.text: Is that the program where all those puppets live in the barrio?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sesame Street
jokes.punchline: Michael's culturally insensitive mischaracterization
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cultural ignorance delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Mmm-hmm. I love that show.
jokes.setup: Michael's incorrect description of Sesame Street
jokes.punchline: Meredith agreeing enthusiastically with the wrong description
jokes.explanation: Meredith's casual agreement with Michael's ignorance

jokes.text: Angela, this is inappropriate. This is my favorite day.
jokes.setup: Angela complaining about Cookie Monster video
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself by calling it her favorite day
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela hypocrisy - condemning something while enjoying it

jokes.text: people better step up and appreciate the crap out of Erin.
jokes.setup: Andy's passionate defense of Secretary's Day
jokes.punchline: The aggressive phrasing 'appreciate the crap out of'
jokes.explanation: Andy's intense devotion expressed in crude but endearing terms

jokes.text: If it wasn't for secretaries, I wouldn't have a stepmom.
jokes.setup: Andy expressing appreciation for secretaries
jokes.punchline: The implication that his father met/left his mother for a secretary
jokes.explanation: Dark family history revealed casually and economically - classic Office darkness

jokes.text: It rocked. It rocked my ass off.
jokes.setup: Pam describing maternity leave
jokes.punchline: The unexpectedly crude intensification
jokes.explanation: Pam using uncharacteristically strong language for emphasis

jokes.text: I literally did not know that existed until this moment. Well, I knew it existed, and I chose to let it die.
jokes.setup: Jim claiming ignorance about the plant
jokes.punchline: Pam immediately revealing his deliberate neglect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim-Pam dynamic with immediate contradiction revealing truth

jokes.text: She's kind of a rube.
jokes.setup: Michael discussing Erin with her boyfriend Andy
jokes.punchline: Calling Andy's girlfriend a rube to his face
jokes.explanation: Michael's social tone-deafness creating immediate awkwardness

jokes.text: That's my girlfriend you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Erin a rube
jokes.punchline: Andy's defensive but restrained response
jokes.explanation: Andy's awkward position defending Erin from Michael

jokes.text: Well, happy boss's day. There wouldn't be a secretary if there was no boss.
jokes.setup: Michael wishing Erin happy Secretary's Day
jokes.punchline: Erin's backwards logic about the hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Erin's naive logic creating unintentional humor

jokes.text: I got a picture of you asking me to lunch.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Erin to lunch
jokes.punchline: Erin treating it as a photo opportunity
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent excitement about mundane moments

jokes.text: Hayworth's is more business-casual, and they always screw up your order
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Hayworth's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Immediately talking himself out of his own suggestion
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-sabotaging tendency in social situations

jokes.text: Yeah, ha, ha, tray of cookies. I'm not falling for that.
jokes.setup: Someone offering Kevin cookies on his birthday
jokes.punchline: Kevin's paranoid assumption it's another Cookie Monster joke
jokes.explanation: Kevin's paranoia about being mocked affecting his birthday celebration

jokes.text: That's some stone-cold narcissism right there.
jokes.setup: Darryl bringing cookies for his own birthday
jokes.punchline: Darryl's self-aware commentary on his own action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Darryl deadpan self-awareness with great word choice

jokes.text: But in a strange way, it feels like they're making fun of me.
jokes.setup: Kevin understanding the Cookie Monster parody is about Cookie Monster
jokes.punchline: His realization that it's actually about him
jokes.explanation: Kevin's slow realization creating both sympathy and comedy

jokes.text: And it really makes me self-conscious about my voice. That's awful. I'm not crying.
jokes.setup: Kevin discussing how the parody affects him
jokes.punchline: Denying crying while clearly emotional
jokes.explanation: Kevin's vulnerability making the humor both funny and uncomfortable

jokes.text: Either they don't respect me, or they respect me too much.
jokes.setup: Gabe describing his authority problems
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of both extremes being problems
jokes.explanation: Gabe's overthinking creating absurd false dichotomy

jokes.text: And some of them still think that I'm the IT guy.
jokes.setup: Gabe discussing his authority issues
jokes.punchline: People not knowing his actual job
jokes.explanation: Gabe's lack of presence and authority in the office

jokes.text: Not to be scary, but, yeah, I would listen to me.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to assert authority about Cookie Monster video
jokes.punchline: The ineffective attempt at intimidation
jokes.explanation: Gabe's failed attempt at authority with awkward phrasing

jokes.text: This is a novelization of the movie Precious, Based on the Book Push by Sapphire.
jokes.setup: Erin wanting to listen to an audiobook
jokes.punchline: The unnecessarily complex description of an adaptation of an adaptation
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent over-explanation of media adaptations

jokes.text: I would have chosen the 1490s. 'Cause America was discovered.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what decade she'd want to be a teenager in
jokes.punchline: Choosing the 1490s for a historically questionable reason
jokes.explanation: Erin's naive historical perspective and odd priorities

jokes.text: Then it became a full Taco Bell and, I don't know, I couldn't keep up.
jokes.setup: Erin describing her job at Taco Bell Express
jokes.punchline: Being overwhelmed by the transition to full Taco Bell
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent admission of being overwhelmed by fast food complexity

jokes.text: At my foster home I never had a desk, so it's like... I don't mean that I didn't like my foster home. I did like it. I just didn't have a desk there.
jokes.setup: Erin explaining what she likes about being a receptionist
jokes.punchline: The awkward over-correction about her foster home
jokes.explanation: Erin's social awkwardness and tragic backstory creating uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: Like, I liked April when I was seven.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about favorite ages or months
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and random answer
jokes.explanation: Erin's literal interpretation of abstract questions

jokes.text: Erin is just weird. How many pillows do you sleep on at night?
jokes.setup: Michael calling Erin weird
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by Erin asking an odd question
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing proving Michael's point immediately

jokes.text: I don't think he is a snappy dresser. Reminds me of Easter.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Andy's fashion sense
jokes.punchline: The random Easter comparison
jokes.explanation: Michael's bizarre fashion criticism with inexplicable holiday reference

jokes.text: Probably didn't want you to have a mental image of him having sex with somebody else. They had sex?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why Andy didn't tell Erin about Angela
jokes.punchline: Erin's shocked realization they had sex
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocence being shattered by Michael's explanation

jokes.text: Yeah, I asked for pickles on my burger, and there are only, like, five or six. Can I get some more pickles?
jokes.setup: Michael complaining to the waiter
jokes.punchline: Five or six pickles somehow being insufficient
jokes.explanation: Michael's unreasonable restaurant demands

jokes.text: In the foster home, my hair was my room.
jokes.setup: Erin having a breakdown at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: The tragic and bizarre explanation for her hair-pulling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy line revealing Erin's tragic past with perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: I'll have what she's having.
jokes.setup: Erin having a very public breakdown
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate When Harry Met Sally reference
jokes.explanation: Michael's tone-deaf movie reference in completely wrong context

jokes.text: Her milk is coming in. She's getting uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Pam appearing uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Dwight's clinical assessment of breastfeeding issues
jokes.explanation: Dwight's matter-of-fact medical knowledge in awkward context

jokes.text: Same thing happens with my cows if I don't tend them frequently enough. You gotta milk them, or else they'll moo like crazy.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pam's breastfeeding discomfort
jokes.punchline: Dwight comparing Pam to his cows
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight inappropriate comparison with perfect character integration

jokes.text: Three squeezes and I would drain you.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering to help with Pam's breastfeeding issue
jokes.punchline: The inappropriately confident and specific offer
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable Dwight line with perfect economy and character consistency

jokes.text: I just like the way it feels.
jokes.setup: Meredith caught using Pam's breast pump
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission of enjoying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith inappropriate behavior with disturbing casualness

jokes.text: This is like the Cadillac of pumps.
jokes.setup: Meredith defending using Pam's breast pump
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a luxury product review
jokes.explanation: Meredith's inappropriate product endorsement of medical equipment

jokes.text: We're both girls. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Pam being horrified about the breast pump being unsterile
jokes.punchline: Meredith's dismissive gender-based logic
jokes.explanation: Meredith's complete lack of hygiene awareness

jokes.text: God, Oscar, will you keep your pants on? It's easy.
jokes.setup: Oscar urgently asking for fax cover sheets
jokes.punchline: Erin using 'keep your pants on' inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Erin's misuse of common expressions

jokes.text: Michael, can I have an advance on my paycheck, because a Mrs. Fields Cookies just opened up at the mall!
jokes.setup: Jim doing a Kevin impression
jokes.punchline: The specific cookie-related financial emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin impression with believable financial priorities

jokes.text: Sorry, Gabe, but that show hasn't been on in many cookies.
jokes.setup: Gabe mentioning MADtv
jokes.punchline: Jim's Cookie Monster-style response
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay continuing the Cookie Monster theme while mocking Gabe

jokes.text: I think we can all agree that Angela's not so great, so...
jokes.setup: Andy praising Angela's party planning
jokes.punchline: Erin's bitter interruption
jokes.explanation: Erin's jealousy creating public awkwardness

jokes.text: that 115-pound moonbeam over there named Erin Hannon.
jokes.setup: Andy giving a romantic speech about Erin
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific weight and 'moonbeam' description
jokes.explanation: Andy's awkward romantic language with creepy weight specificity

jokes.text: in addition to being the person who brings me my fax confirmations.
jokes.setup: Andy describing Erin as his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Adding the mundane job description
jokes.explanation: Andy's inability to separate romance from office dynamics

jokes.text: I know about Angela. I know that you were engaged to her and that you were sleeping with her. Michael told me.
jokes.setup: Andy about to perform his romantic song
jokes.punchline: Erin's public confrontation ruining the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for maximum awkwardness and public humiliation

jokes.text: Did you sleep with Phyllis or Kelly or Pam? Maybe all together?
jokes.setup: Erin confronting Andy about Angela
jokes.punchline: Escalating to group sex accusations
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent paranoia leading to absurd accusations

jokes.text: Who are you? I don't know you. Get away!
jokes.setup: Erin's public breakdown continuing
jokes.punchline: Treating Andy like a stranger after dating for weeks
jokes.explanation: Erin's dramatic overreaction creating public spectacle

jokes.text: I should probably get involved in this, but I think my energy is better spent on the Cookie Monster issue.
jokes.setup: Major relationship drama happening at the party
jokes.punchline: Gabe prioritizing the Cookie Monster video over real problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe moment showing his complete lack of priority sense

jokes.text: Hey, Erin, you look delicious! I mean, beautiful.
jokes.setup: Pam doing a Kevin impression
jokes.punchline: The Freudian slip from 'delicious' to 'beautiful'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin impression with food-focused mindset

jokes.text: I'm going to have to suspend you without pay for two days.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to assert authority over Cookie Monster impressions
jokes.punchline: The absurdly harsh punishment for a harmless joke
jokes.explanation: Gabe's complete overreaction showing his insecurity

jokes.text: Wait, what? I was just slow-clapping your no-nonsense decision-making.
jokes.setup: Gabe suspending Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confused defense of his supportive gesture
jokes.explanation: Dwight's sarcastic support being misinterpreted by paranoid Gabe

jokes.text: C is for suspension.
jokes.setup: Getting suspended for Cookie Monster impressions
jokes.punchline: Cookie Monster-style response to the suspension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim rebellion using Cookie Monster format

jokes.text: Everyone here knows that I can't and won't keep a secret.
jokes.setup: Michael defending himself for revealing Andy's secret
jokes.punchline: His proud admission of being untrustworthy
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete lack of self-awareness about his character flaws

jokes.text: It's the kind of thing you wish you could have annulled.
jokes.setup: Angela talking about her relationship with Andy
jokes.punchline: Wishing she could annul a non-marriage
jokes.explanation: Angela's extreme disgust with past relationship

jokes.text: Sometimes the heart doesn't know what it wants until it finds what it wants.
jokes.setup: Pam giving Erin relationship advice
jokes.punchline: The circular logic disguised as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Well-meaning but ultimately meaningless relationship advice

jokes.text: And I gotta go home and I got to adjust it.
jokes.setup: Jim talking about taking his suspension to fix his attitude
jokes.punchline: The mechanical way he describes attitude adjustment
jokes.explanation: Jim's mock-serious approach to his fake punishment

jokes.text: I kneel before you.
jokes.setup: Dwight apologizing to Gabe
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic physical gesture
jokes.explanation: Dwight's medieval approach to workplace apologies

jokes.text: Yeah, my chest is not naturally hairless, and my parents pay my credit card bill.
jokes.setup: Andy promising no more secrets
jokes.punchline: Immediately sharing embarrassing personal details
jokes.explanation: Andy's oversharing in attempt to be honest

jokes.text: Like that girl Precious in Precious, Based on the Novel Push by Sapphire. Okay?
jokes.setup: Erin saying she needs to be alone
jokes.punchline: The unnecessarily complex movie reference again
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier audiobook description, showing Erin's consistency

jokes.text: Or cookies!
jokes.setup: Someone offering to buy everyone coffee
jokes.punchline: Kevin's hopeful cookie addition
jokes.explanation: Kevin's food-focused addition to coffee offer

jokes.text: Guys, I'm now going to apologize for the mess that we're in, because corporate told me to.
jokes.setup: Jim doing a Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: The admission that corporate forced the apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe impression revealing his corporate puppet nature

jokes.text: I just want you to know, I laugh like a crazy person.
jokes.setup: Jim continuing Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on Gabe's distinctive laugh
jokes.explanation: Accurate observation about Gabe's mannerisms

jokes.text: I have to go back to the zoo to the stick insect exhibit.
jokes.setup: Jim doing Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: Comparing Gabe to a stick insect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy comparing Gabe's appearance to insects

jokes.text: I say ciao 'cause I'm fancy from Tallahassee.
jokes.setup: Jim mocking Gabe's Italian goodbye
jokes.punchline: The contradiction between 'fancy' and Tallahassee
jokes.explanation: Perfect mockery of Gabe's pretentious vs. actual background

jokes.text: I don't think she's ever pooped in her life.
jokes.setup: Michael listing Angela's supposed flaws
jokes.punchline: The bizarre bowel movement observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate overshare disguised as comfort

jokes.text: Andy, his butt looks big in those khakis. Oh, I like his butt.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Andy's appearance
jokes.punchline: Erin's defensive response about liking his butt
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing with Michael's plan backfiring immediately

jokes.text: But Oscar is an accountant, and the Count counts numbers. You're an accountant, too, so it doesn't...
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to explain Oscar's Count impression
jokes.punchline: Getting confused by his own logic
jokes.explanation: Kevin's attempt at logic falling apart mid-sentence

jokes.text: Why didn't you do Oscar the Grouch? That would have been funny.
jokes.setup: Kevin critiquing Oscar's Sesame Street impression choice
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the name-based connection
jokes.explanation: Kevin having a moment of comedic clarity about impressions

tmdb_overview: Michael gets kicked out of the bar when he pulls out all the stops to impress a friend of Jim and Pam's. Meanwhile, Andy and Erin do their best to keep their relationship under wraps. Dwight rethinks his pre-natal contract with Angela.
